metadata.title: Accurate And Scalable Estimation Of Epistemic Uncertainty For Graph Neural Networks

sections.title: Stochastic Centering: Single Model Ensembles
content: Stochastic Centering creates arelative representationfor an input, x, in terms of a randomanchor, c.
During training, the anchor israndomized, thus implicitly sampling different hypotheses.
During testing, predictions are computed overmultipleanchors.

figures.description: Training manifold visualization with anchors and test samples

sections.title: G Δ-UQ: Stochastic Centering for GNNs
content: Since graphs are variable sized and discrete, we introduce three anchoring strategies tailored for GNNs.
Inducevarying levels of stochasticity, trading off hypothesis diversity and the semantic diversity of the anchoring distribution.

tables.description: GNN architecture diagram showing node features and readout layers

sections.title: We propose G Δ-UQ, a novel training paradigm for improving calibration with GNNs
Improved Node Classification Calibration
content: We evaluate G Δ-UQ with node feature anchoring on:
G Δ-UQ matches or surpasses accuracy and calibration of the vanilla GNN on 8/8 datasets.

sections.title: G Δ-UQ Improves Graph Classification Calibration with Different MPNNs
content: G Δ-UQ is flexible to message passing layer. Required level of stochasticity may vary for dataset/GNN.
